productStatement: GrantLayer turns agentic grant workflows into verifiable institutional records.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Secret source abstraction
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Abstracts secret storage and retrieval so the Product Core does not depend on one specific vault or secret manager.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: secret category identifier and runtime mode
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: resolved secret value or explicit failure with no secret leakage
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must support multiple secret managers and environment boundaries; must validate source on startup
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Future adapters may include HashiCorp Vault AWS Secrets Manager Azure Key Vault and Google Secret Manager.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Secret configuration schema
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Defines a validated schema that declares required secrets their sources and rotation expectations.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: validated secret configuration with explicit required and optional entries
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must refuse startup if required secret configuration is missing invalid or uses placeholders
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Schema must be versioned and validated before any production claim.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Runtime secret source validation
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Validates secret sources are reachable and returning valid values on startup and periodically.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: secret configuration and active runtime mode
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: startup allowed or explicit structured error with no secret leakage
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must refuse startup if required secret sources are unreachable or return invalid values
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Validation must not expose secret values in error messages or logs.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Production fail-closed secret enforcement
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Ensures production mode refuses to start if required secret configuration is missing or invalid.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: runtime mode secret configuration and secret source state
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: startup allowed or explicit structured error with no secret leakage
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must refuse startup if any required secret is missing invalid or uses demo defaults
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Fail-closed behavior must be validated by integration tests before any production claim.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Secret redaction helper
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Masks or omits secret-bearing fields from logs diagnostics exports and API responses.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: structured output that may contain secret-bearing fields
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: sanitized output with secret values replaced by redaction markers
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must redact all secret values in production logs diagnostics and exports without exception
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Redaction must be deterministic and tested across all output surfaces.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Signing and key management abstraction
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Abstracts key storage rotation and access for Ed25519 or future signing algorithms.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: key identifier and signing operation request
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: signature or public key material without exposing private key
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must use explicit managed key source with rotation support; no synthetic keypairs
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Software signing is acceptable for pilots. HSM is a future upgrade path.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Database credential configuration
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Sources database credentials through the secret boundary with masking in logs and error messages.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: database backend identifier and connection context
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: validated connection string or credential set with no plaintext exposure
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must source credentials through secret boundary; must mask in all logs and errors
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Connection strings must never appear in diagnostics or error output.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Auth provider secret configuration
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Sources auth provider secrets through the secret boundary with validation and rotation expectations.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: auth provider type and runtime mode
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: validated auth provider secret set with no plaintext exposure
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must source secrets through secret boundary; must validate on startup; must support rotation
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Auth provider secrets must not appear in any log audit record or evidence bundle.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Evidence storage credential configuration
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Sources evidence storage credentials through the secret boundary where external storage is used.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: evidence storage backend identifier and runtime mode
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: validated storage credential with no plaintext exposure in evidence metadata
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must source credentials through secret boundary; must not leak into evidence bundle metadata
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Evidence storage credentials must be scoped to storage operations only.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Observability sink secret configuration
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Sources observability backend credentials through the secret boundary with redaction in startup output.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: observability backend identifier and runtime mode
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: validated sink credential with no plaintext exposure in startup or health output
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must source credentials through secret boundary; must not appear in startup logs or health checks
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Observability credentials must not appear in health check responses or startup banners.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: External integration secret adapter
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Translates partner and third-party secret requirements into Product Core secret boundary calls.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: external integration identifier and required secret categories
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: scoped credential set mapped to the Product Core secret boundary
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must be explicit and scoped; must support rotation on partner change
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Each external integration should have its own adapter that does not leak secrets into Product Core records.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Local test demo shortcut guard
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Prevents local test and demo placeholder secrets from being accepted in production.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: runtime mode and secret value or identifier
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: allowed or rejected with explicit non-production labeling
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must reject all placeholder identifiers and demo secrets in production mode
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Shortcut guard must be tested by integration tests before any production claim.

secretHandlingBoundaries.name: Secret leakage prevention tests
secretHandlingBoundaries.purpose: Validates that secrets do not appear in logs exports responses or fixtures under any runtime mode.
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedInputs: test suite output and fixture inventory
secretHandlingBoundaries.expectedOutputs: pass or fail with specific leakage location if found
secretHandlingBoundaries.productionRequirement: must run in CI and block merge if any leakage is detected
secretHandlingBoundaries.notes: Leakage prevention tests must cover all output surfaces including logs errors exports and responses.

failClosedRules: Production mode must refuse to start if required secret source is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if any required secret is missing or returns an invalid value.
Production mode must refuse to start if demo or default admin tokens are present.
Production mode must refuse to start if placeholder secrets or synthetic keypairs are detected.
Production mode must refuse to start if secret-bearing configuration is incomplete or unvalidated.
Production mode must refuse to start if local test or demo shortcut guard is disabled or bypassed.
Production mode must refuse to start if secrets are detected in source code fixtures or committed configuration.
Missing or invalid secret configuration must produce a clear structured error without leaking secrets.
redactionRules: Secret-bearing configuration must be redacted in startup logs health checks and diagnostics.
Secret values must be replaced by a deterministic redaction marker such as REDACTED.
Connection strings containing credentials must be masked in all output surfaces.
Audit records must never contain plaintext tokens keys or credentials.
Error messages from external integrations must be sanitized before inclusion in logs or responses.
Config dumps and diagnostic endpoints must omit or mask all secret-bearing fields.
Secret source names may be logged but secret values must not.

localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: Synthetic labels like demo-admin or test-operator that carry no real secret value.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Placeholder identifiers provide no authentication and must not be accepted in production.

localDemoTestShortcuts.shortcut: Local-only demo admin token references without real secret values
localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: References that describe where a token would come from not the token itself.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Token references without real values bypass authentication and must not be used in production.

localDemoTestShortcuts.shortcut: Test-only fake identity labels that are not credentials
localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: Fixture names that are clearly not real accounts or passwords.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Fake identities provide no real authentication and must not be present in production.

localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: File paths for local development that contain no credentials.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: SQLite paths are acceptable locally but lack production durability and scaling.

localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: Deterministic synthetic identifiers used in test fixtures.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Fixture IDs are synthetic and must not be mixed with institutional data in production.

localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: Integrations that are explicitly turned off requiring no live credentials.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Disabled integrations bypass external validation and must not be deployed to production without explicit enablement.

localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: Manifests that simulate secret resolution without real values.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Dry-run manifests do not validate real secret sources and must not be used in production.

productionRequiredConfiguration: Explicit secret source manager or validated environment boundary
Explicit database credential source with masking rules
Explicit auth provider credential source with validation rules
Explicit signing and key management source with rotation expectations
Explicit evidence storage credential source where external storage is used
Explicit observability sink credential source with redaction rules
Explicit external integration credential sources scoped per integration
Secret redaction helper enabled and verified
Local test demo shortcut guard enabled and verified
Fail-closed startup check for missing or invalid secrets
Leakage prevention tests running in CI and blocking merge on detection
leakagePreventionExpectations: Secrets must not appear in committed source code.
Secrets must not appear in docs examples or OpenAPI examples.
Secrets must not appear in JSON fixtures or test output.
Secrets must not appear in logs startup output or diagnostics.
Secrets must not appear in auditor exports or compliance readiness summaries.
Secrets must not appear in evidence bundle metadata or provenance records.
Secrets must not appear in error messages or external integration failure output.
Secrets must not appear in git history screenshots or shared artifacts.
Leakage prevention tests must cover all output surfaces including logs errors exports and responses.
CI must block merge if any secret leakage is detected.
implementationNonGoals: OAuth JWT SSO secret handling implementation
